answer.text: A list of high-risk jurisdictions for which FATF calls on all members to apply enhanced due diligence and, in serious cases, counter-measures
A list of countries permanently banned from the international financial system
A list of banks that have been found guilty of money laundering
A list of countries that are not members of FATF

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** For all countries identified as high-risk by FATF (the 'black list'), FATF calls on all members and urges all jurisdictions to apply enhanced due diligence. In the most serious cases, countries are called upon to apply counter-measures to protect the international financial system from ML/TF/PF risks emanating from that country.
**The Trap:** Black list doesn't mean permanent ban — countries can be removed from the black list if they address deficiencies. Also, non-membership of FATF doesn't automatically mean black listing.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Black list implications are critical for IFSCA-01 because IFSC entities deal with international clients. Enhanced due diligence or counter-measures for black-listed country clients are mandatory.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Black list = MANDATORY enhanced due diligence, possible counter-measures. This is a regulatory requirement, not a choice. Dealing with clients from black-listed jurisdictions requires the highest level of scrutiny.
